readme:
# Schmancy

A Web Component UI library built on Lit, RxJS, and Tailwind CSS. Surfaces are glass. Depth is light. Interactions are physics.

## Agent runtime

For sandboxed-iframe agents (Claude Design, Claude Artifacts, any LLM that can
only write HTML), schmancy ships a single-URL runtime at `@mhmo91/schmancy/agent`.
Drop one `<script type="module">` tag and every `<schmancy-*>` element is
registered. No bundler, no bare specifiers, no npm install.

```html
<script type="module">
  import { $dialog, theme } from 'https://esm.sh/@mhmo91/schmancy/agent';
</script>
<schmancy-theme root scheme="dark">
  <schmancy-surface type="solid" fill="all">
    <schmancy-button>Hi</schmancy-button>
    <schmancy-skill></schmancy-skill>
  </schmancy-surface>
</schmancy-theme>
```

The `<schmancy-skill>` tag installs `window.schmancy` for runtime discovery:

- `window.schmancy.help()` — full manifest (CEM v1 shape).
- `window.schmancy.help('schmancy-button')` — one tag's attributes, events, slots, CSS parts.
- `window.schmancy.tokens()` — build-time-extracted list of `--schmancy-*` theme tokens.
- `window.schmancy.manifestUrl` — Blob URL; `fetch()` it for the same data.
- `window.schmancy.a11yAudit()` — walks the live DOM and reports ARIA / shadow-root / form-association status per instance.
- `window.schmancy.platformPrimitive('schmancy-dialog')` — map to the native element a component wraps (present when the component's JSDoc has `@platform`).
- `window.schmancy.capabilities()` — runtime feature probe (`popover`, `declarativeShadowDom`, `scopedRegistries`, `trustedTypes`, `cssRegisteredProperties`, `elementInternalsAria`, `formAssociated`, `adoptedStyleSheets`). Agents use this to adapt to the sandbox they're in rather than the one they expect.

Every enum-typed attribute carries a `values` array — e.g. `schmancy-button`'s `variant` ships `["elevated", "filled", "filled tonal", "tonal", "outlined", "text"]` so agents never have to parse `"'filled' | 'tonal' | ..."` strings.

The manifest is also emitted as a sibling file at `@mhmo91/schmancy/agent/manifest`
for tooling that prefers reading JSON from disk.

## Install

```bash
npm install @mhmo91/schmancy
```

```typescript
import '@mhmo91/schmancy'
import { magnetic, cursorGlow, gravity } from '@mhmo91/schmancy/directives'
```

## Use with Claude Code

Schmancy ships a Claude Code plugin. In any Claude Code session, run:

```
/plugin install https://github.com/samwaai/schmancy
```

Claude now knows every Schmancy component, foundation pattern, and convention in your project. The skill activates automatically when you work on schmancy code — no CLAUDE.md edits, no symlinks.

## Quick Start

```html
<schmancy-theme root scheme="dark">
  <schmancy-surface type="solid" fill="all">
    <schmancy-area name="root" .default=${lazy(() => import('./home.page'))}>
      <schmancy-route when="home-page" .component=${lazy(() => import('./home.page'))} />
    </schmancy-area>
  </schmancy-surface>
</schmancy-theme>
```

## Design: Luminous Glass

| Surface | Opacity | Blur | Purpose |
|---------|---------|------|---------|
| `solid` | 92% | — | Dense glass, high readability |
| `subtle` | 78% | 8px | Frosted panel (default) |
| `glass` | 55% | 16px | Overlays, dialogs, dropdowns |
| `luminous` | 42% | 20px | Hero panels with glow halo |

## Docs

Schmancy is organized in four layers:

- **Foundations** — [Area](./skills/schmancy/area.md) · [Store](./skills/schmancy/store.md) · [Mixins ($LitElement)](./skills/schmancy/mixins.md) · [Theme](./skills/schmancy/theme.md) · [Directives](./skills/schmancy/directives.md)
- **Atoms** — [Typography](./skills/schmancy/typography.md) · [Icons](./skills/schmancy/icons.md) · [Button](./skills/schmancy/button.md) · [Surface](./skills/schmancy/surface.md) · [Divider](./skills/schmancy/divider.md) · [Avatar](./skills/schmancy/avatar.md)
- **Composites (by job)** — Forms, Navigation, Overlays, Interaction, Feedback, Display
- **Utilities** — [Animation](./skills/schmancy/animation.md) · [Audio](./skills/schmancy/audio.md) · [Discovery](./skills/schmancy/discovery.md) · [RxJS Utils](./skills/schmancy/rxjs-utils.md) · [Utils](./skills/schmancy/utils.md)

**Full component index:** [skills/schmancy/INDEX.md](./skills/schmancy/INDEX.md) — the single-file map with every tag, service, and convention. Written primarily for AI agents; humans welcome.

## Tech Stack

[Lit](https://lit.dev) · [RxJS](https://rxjs.dev) · [Tailwind CSS v4](https://tailwindcss.com) · [Blackbird](./src/utils/animation.ts)

## License

Apache-2.0

declarations.name: SchmancyAvatar
declarations.description: A customizable avatar component that displays initials or an icon Designed to match the Schmancy design system

declarations.name: SchmancySkill
declarations.description: Self-describing runtime helper. Drop `<schmancy-skill></schmancy-skill>` once on a page and `window.schmancy.help('schmancy-button')` returns the machine-readable entry for any tag. Renders nothing.

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: The name of the router outlet

declarations.name: area
declarations.description: Area router singleton. Drives named `<schmancy-area>` outlets in the DOM — imperative push / pop, URL-synced, state-restoring. Pair with `lazy(() => import('./view'))` for code-split routes.
declarations.summary: Imperative outlet-based router for `<schmancy-area>` elements.

methods.summary: Navigate an area to a component.
Pop the top entry off an area's stack.
Observable of the active route state for an area.

declarations.name: SchmancyRoute
declarations.description: A marker component that holds route configuration. This component doesn't render anything - it's used by schmancy-area to configure routing via slot change detection.

declarations.name: SchmancyAutocomplete
declarations.description: Combobox with type-ahead filtering over a list of `<schmancy-option>` children. Single or multi-select. Form-associated.
declarations.summary: Use when users need to pick from a known list of options but the list is too long for a plain select dropdown. Prefer schmancy-select for short static lists.

platformPrimitive.note: Composed of a schmancy-input + a floating listbox populated from `<schmancy-option>` children. Multi-select renders selections as schmancy-input-chip chips. Degrades to a datalist-backed native input if the tag never registers.

examples:
<schmancy-autocomplete name="country" label="Country" placeholder="Start typing…">
  <schmancy-option value="US">United States</schmancy-option>
  <schmancy-option value="CA">Canada</schmancy-option>
  <schmancy-option value="GB">United Kingdom</schmancy-option>
</schmancy-autocomplete>

attributes.name: color
attributes.description: The color variant of the badge

attributes.name: size
attributes.description: The size of the badge

attributes.name: shape
attributes.description: The shape of the badge

attributes.name: outlined
attributes.description: Whether the badge has an outlined style

attributes.name: icon
attributes.description: Custom icon name to display (if no icon slot is provided)

attributes.name: pulse
attributes.description: Whether to make the badge pulse to draw attention

slots.description: The content of the badge (text or HTML)

slots.name: icon
slots.description: Optional icon to display before the content

attributes.name: expandedWidth
attributes.description: Override the expanded panel width (e.g. '320px', '24rem'). Defaults to responsive sizing.

attributes.name: lowered
attributes.description: When true, uses a lower elevation shadow in the minimized (FAB) state.

attributes.name: corner
attributes.description: Corner the boat is anchored to.

attributes.name: open
attributes.description: Whether the panel is open.

declarations.name: SchmancyBreadcrumb
declarations.description: Breadcrumb trail — navigation history from root to current page. Renders schmancy-breadcrumb-item children with separators between.
declarations.summary: Use for deep hierarchical navigation (file explorer paths, e-commerce category chains, admin settings trees). Last item is styled as the current page automatically.

platformPrimitive.note: Styled `<nav aria-label="Breadcrumb">`. Degrades to a plain nav if the tag never registers.

declarations.name: SchmancyBreadcrumbItem
declarations.description: Single segment in a schmancy-breadcrumb trail — a link when `href` is set, or a plain span (the current page) when omitted.
declarations.summary: Always nested inside schmancy-breadcrumb. Omit `href` on the current page — it gets aria-current="page" automatically.

platformPrimitive.note: Renders an `<a>` or `<span>` depending on href. Degrades to a plain anchor/span if the tag never registers.

attributes.name: size
attributes.description: Size of the spinner - M3 aligned tokens or numeric Tailwind units Tokens: 'xxs' (12px), 'xs' (16px), 'sm' (20px), 'md' (24px), 'lg' (32px) Numeric: Tailwind units where each unit = 4px (e.g., 6 = 24px)

declarations.name: SchmancyButton
declarations.description: Material Design button — primary interactive surface for triggering actions or navigation.
declarations.summary: Trigger actions or navigate. Form-associated; participates in native form submission.

platformPrimitive.note: Schmancy-skinned native `<button type="submit">`. When `href` is set, degrades to `<a href="…">`. Falls back to plain `<button>` styled with Tailwind if the tag never registers.

attributes.name: variant
attributes.description: The variant of the button.

attributes.name: color
attributes.description: The color of the button.

attributes.name: width
attributes.description: The width of the button.

attributes.name: size
attributes.description: The size of the button. Provided as `SchmancyButtonSizeContext` to descendant elements so children (e.g. `<schmancy-icon>`) can auto-size against the enclosing button.

attributes.name: type
attributes.description: The type of the button. Defaults to 'button' (preventing accidental form submissions).

attributes.name: href
attributes.description: The URL the button points to. If provided, the component will render as an anchor element.

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Determines whether the button is disabled.

cssParts.name: base
cssParts.description: The underlying native `<button>` (or `<a>` when `href` is set).

declarations.name: SchmnacyIconButton
declarations.description: Icon-only button for toolbar actions, close affordances, or overflow menus.
declarations.summary: Compact round/square button wrapping a single icon glyph. Form-associated like schmancy-button.

platformPrimitive.note: Schmancy-skinned native `<button>` (or `<a>` when `href` is set). aria-label is required for a11y because there's no text content.

attributes.name: size
attributes.description: The size of the icon.

attributes.name: variant
attributes.description: The variant of the button.

attributes.name: width
attributes.description: The width of the button.

attributes.name: type
attributes.description: The type of the button. Defaults to 'button' (preventing accidental form submissions).

attributes.name: href
attributes.description: The URL the button points to. If provided, the component will render as an anchor element.

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Determines whether the button is disabled.

attributes.name: text
attributes.description: Render slot content as text instead of wrapping in schmancy-icon. Use this when you want to display text labels in the button.

attributes.name: icon
attributes.description: Icon name - use this instead of slot content to prevent translation breaking icons. Example: <schmancy-icon-button icon="add"></schmancy-icon-button>

slots.description: The default slot (usually an icon or glyph).

cssParts.name: base
cssParts.description: The underlying native `<button>` (or `<a>` when `href` is set).

declarations.name: SchmancyCardAction
declarations.description: Action row of a schmancy-card — holds the card's buttons / links (typically aligned bottom-right).
declarations.summary: Always nested inside schmancy-card. Holds the primary + secondary CTAs for the card.

platformPrimitive.note: Styled flex container. Degrades to a plain div if the tag never registers.

declarations.name: SchmancyCard
declarations.description: Material Design card — a surface-level container for grouping related content with media / content / actions slots.
declarations.summary: Use for discrete pieces of content that appear in a list (a product, a note, a message). Combine with schmancy-card-media / schmancy-card-content / schmancy-card-action children.

platformPrimitive.note: Styled `<div>`; becomes an `<a>` when `href` is set so the whole card is a single interactive surface. Degrades to a plain div/a if the tag never registers.

attributes.name: type
attributes.description: The visual style of the card.

attributes.name: interactive
attributes.description: Makes the card interactive (clickable).

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Disables the card.

attributes.name: dragged
attributes.description: Indicates the card is being dragged.

attributes.name: href
attributes.description: URL to navigate to when card is clicked (makes it act like a link).

attributes.name: target
attributes.description: Target for the link navigation.

attributes.name: role
attributes.description: ARIA role for accessibility.

attributes.name: aria-label
attributes.description: ARIA label for accessibility.

examples:
Title

One-line description of the card's content.

 Open

declarations.name: SchmancyCardContent
declarations.description: Content region of a schmancy-card — holds the card's headline, supporting text, and inline controls.
declarations.summary: Always nested inside schmancy-card. The padded content block between the media and action rows.

platformPrimitive.note: Styled `<div>` with padding. Degrades to a plain div if the tag never registers.

examples:
Card title

Supporting text that describes the card's subject.

declarations.name: SchmancyCardMedia
declarations.description: Media region of a schmancy-card — the image / thumbnail at the top of the card.
declarations.summary: Always nested inside schmancy-card. Pass `src` + `alt` props or slot an `` / `

platformPrimitive.note: Renders an `` (or wraps a slotted media element). Degrades to a styled `` if the tag never registers.

attributes.description: Chart data points

attributes.name: height
attributes.description: Chart height in pixels

attributes.name: showGrid
attributes.description: Show grid lines

attributes.name: showLabels
attributes.description: Show x-axis labels

attributes.name: showTooltip
attributes.description: Enable tooltips

attributes.name: peakCount

attributes.name: animationDuration

attributes.name: valueDecimals
attributes.description: Decimal places for value display

attributes.name: theme
attributes.description: Theme overrides

attributes.name: data
attributes.description: Chart data points

attributes.name: valuePrefix
attributes.description: Prefix for values (e.g., "EUR ")

attributes.name: valueSuffix
attributes.description: Suffix for values (e.g., "%")

attributes.name: valueDecimals
attributes.description: Decimal places for value display

attributes.name: showMedals
attributes.description: Show medals for top 3

attributes.name: showLegend
attributes.description: Show legend for segments

attributes.name: animationDuration
attributes.description: Animation duration in milliseconds

attributes.name: animated
attributes.description: Enable entrance animation

attributes.name: labelWidth
attributes.description: Label column width (Tailwind class)

attributes.name: valueWidth
attributes.description: Value column width (Tailwind class)

declarations.name: SchmancyCheckboxElement
declarations.description: Binary checkbox for multi-select or boolean form fields. Wraps Material Web's `<md-checkbox>`; form-associated.
declarations.summary: Use for "select many from a list" or any boolean that's part of a form submission. Prefer schmancy-switch for immediate-effect toggles.

platformPrimitive.note: Wraps `<md-checkbox>` from `@material/web`. Degrades to styled native `<input type="checkbox">` if the tag never registers.

events.name: valueChange
events.description: `CustomEvent<{ value: boolean }>` when the checkbox is toggled.

declarations.name: SchmancyAssistChip
declarations.description: Assist chip component - prompts user actions like opening calendar events or sharing content Pure Schmancy implementation with Tailwind CSS and RxJS state management

attributes.name: value
attributes.description: Value identifier for the chip

attributes.name: icon
attributes.description: Optional icon name (Material Symbols)

attributes.name: href
attributes.description: Optional href for navigation

attributes.name: target
attributes.description: Target for navigation (e.g., '_blank')

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Disable the chip

attributes.name: elevated
attributes.description: Elevated style variant - true by default per M3 spec for assist chips

declarations.name: SchmancyChips
declarations.description: Filter-chip group — container for selectable `<schmancy-chip>` children. Single or multi-select.
declarations.summary: Use for filtering or choosing from 2–8 mutually-visible options ("Status: active / paused / archived"). Prefer schmancy-select when the list gets long or vertical.

platformPrimitive.note: No direct native equivalent. Degrades to a styled schmancy-select with similar semantics if the tag never registers.

examples:
<schmancy-chips multi @change=${(e) => this.filters = e.detail.values}>
  <schmancy-chip value="active">Active</schmancy-chip>
  <schmancy-chip value="paused">Paused</schmancy-chip>
  <schmancy-chip value="archived">Archived</schmancy-chip>
</schmancy-chips>

declarations.name: SchmancyInputChip
declarations.description: Input chip — displays user-provided information (tags, recipients, filters) that can be removed but not toggled.  IMPORTANT: Per Material Design 3 specification, input chips do NOT have selected state. They represent discrete pieces of user input (like entered tags, selections from lists, etc.) that can only be removed, not toggled on/off.  Use cases: - Displaying selected recipients in an email - Showing applied filters that can be removed - Tags or keywords entered by the user - Selected items from a multi-select dropdown
declarations.summary: Removable pill that represents a single user input value. No selected state — use schmancy-chip (filter chip) for toggleable options.

platformPrimitive.note: No native equivalent. Composed of a labeled pill + close button. Degrades to a styled `<span>` with a trailing close `<button>` if the tag never registers.

attributes.name: value
attributes.description: Value identifier for the chip

attributes.name: icon
attributes.description: Optional icon name (Material Symbols)

attributes.name: avatar
attributes.description: Optional avatar image URL

attributes.name: removable
attributes.description: Whether to show remove button (default true for input chips)

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Disable the chip

attributes.name: elevated
attributes.description: Elevated style variant

events.name: click
events.description: Optional click event on chip body (value)

events.name: remove
events.description: Dispatched when remove button is clicked (value)

declarations.name: SchmancySuggestionChip
declarations.description: Suggestion chip component - provides contextual recommendations to users  IMPORTANT: Suggestion chips do NOT have a selected state. They are designed to provide suggestions and recommendations that trigger actions when clicked. Unlike filter chips, they cannot be toggled on/off.  Pure Schmancy implementation with Tailwind CSS and RxJS state management

attributes.description: Value identifier for the chip

attributes.name: icon

attributes.name: href

attributes.description: Target for navigation (e.g., '_blank')

attributes.description: Disable the chip

attributes.name: elevated
attributes.description: Elevated style variant - flat by default per M3 spec

attributes.name: language

attributes.name: code

attributes.name: filename
attributes.description: Optional filename or title to display in header

attributes.name: lineNumbers
attributes.description: Show line numbers

attributes.name: copyButton

attributes.name: highlightLines
