attributes.name: language
attributes.description: Programming language for syntax highlighting

attributes.name: layout
attributes.description: Show code on top or side-by-side

attributes.name: preview
attributes.description: Whether to render/execute the code in preview section When false, only shows the code without rendering

declarations.name: SchmancyConnectivityStatus
examples:
<!-- Add once to your app root -->
<schmancy-connectivity-status></schmancy-connectivity-status>

declarations.name: schmancyContentDrawer
declarations.description: Content-drawer service. Mounts any element inside the right-hand content drawer of a `<schmancy-content-drawer>` layout.
declarations.summary: Imperative content-drawer (right-side navigation panel).

methods.summary: Mount an element in the drawer.

attributes.name: open
attributes.description: The state of the sheet

declarations.name: SchmancyDateRangeDialog
declarations.description: Dialog content component for date range selection  Redesigned with chip-based horizontal flow layout: - Quick Select: Today, Yesterday, This Week, This Month - Days: Last 7/14/30/60/90 Days - Periods: Last Week, Last Month, Last Quarter, Last Year - Year to Date: This Week, This Month, This Quarter, This Year, YTD - Custom Range: Manual date inputs at the bottom

declarations.name: SchmancyDateRange
declarations.description: A date range selector that supports presets and manual date input.

type.text: { label: string; dateFrom: string; dateTo: string; }[]

attributes.name: collapse
attributes.description: When true, collapses to just an icon button on mobile screens

events.name: change
events.description: Fired when the date range changes with dateFrom and dateTo values

declarations.name: SchmancyDateRangeInline
declarations.description: Smart inline date range picker that handles all the heavy lifting. Auto-corrects invalid ranges, provides smart defaults, and validates dates.

attributes.name: type
attributes.description: Input type - 'date' or 'datetime-local'

attributes.name: dateFrom
attributes.description: From date configuration

attributes.name: dateTo
attributes.description: To date configuration

attributes.name: minDate
attributes.description: Minimum allowed date

attributes.name: maxDate
attributes.description: Maximum allowed date

attributes.name: compact
attributes.description: Compact mode for smaller UI

attributes.name: autoCorrect
attributes.description: Auto-correct invalid date ranges

attributes.name: minGap
attributes.description: Minimum gap between dates (in days)

attributes.name: maxGap
attributes.description: Maximum gap between dates (in days)

attributes.name: defaultGap
attributes.description: Default gap when auto-setting dates (in days)

attributes.name: allowSameDate
attributes.description: Whether to allow same date selection

declarations.name: SchmancyDetails
declarations.description: Expandable disclosure panel — a styled `<details>` / `<summary>` pair with animated expand + overlay options.
declarations.summary: Use for progressive-disclosure content: FAQs, collapsible settings sections, accordion-style lists. Prefer schmancy-expand for full-page accordions where only one section can be open at a time.

platformPrimitive.note: Wraps native `<details>`/`<summary>`. Degrades to the native element if the tag never registers — same keyboard accessibility, just no animation.

events.name: toggle
events.description: When the open state changes (bubbles from the native `<details>`).

examples: Order ships in 2 business days.

declarations.name: $dialog
declarations.description: Global dialog utility. Imperative modal API — open any element as a dialog, or use the built-in `confirm` / `ask` / `danger` prompts. Returns Promises that resolve when the user picks an action.
declarations.summary: Imperative modal dialogs and built-in confirm/ask/danger prompts.

methods.summary: One-line yes/no prompt anchored to the triggering event.
Destructive-action confirm styled with the error palette.
Open any element as a modal.
Dismiss a dialog by id, or the topmost dialog.

declarations.name: SchmancyDialog
declarations.description: Modal dialog — content-only (just a styled panel) or confirm mode (title + message + confirm/cancel buttons). Prefer the imperative `$dialog` service for most use cases; use the element directly only when you want a declaratively-positioned dialog.
declarations.summary: Blocks interaction with the rest of the page until dismissed. For quick confirmations, prefer `$dialog.confirm({ ... })` over this element. For arbitrary dialog content driven imperatively, prefer `$dialog.component(MyComponent)`.

platformPrimitive.note: Positioned overlay in light DOM. Degrades to a styled `<dialog>` if the tag never registers — loses custom animations but keeps focus trap + ESC close.

attributes.name: uid
attributes.description: Unique identifier for the dialog instance

attributes.name: title
attributes.description: Dialog title (enables confirm mode when set)

attributes.name: subtitle
attributes.description: Dialog subtitle

attributes.name: message
attributes.description: Dialog message

attributes.name: confirm-text
attributes.description: Text for confirm button (enables confirm mode when set with cancelText)

attributes.name: cancel-text
attributes.description: Text for cancel button

attributes.name: variant
attributes.description: Dialog variant (affects button colors in confirm mode)

attributes.name: hide-actions
attributes.description: Whether to hide action buttons (force content mode)

events.name: confirm
events.description: In confirm mode, when the confirm button is clicked.

events.name: cancel
events.description: In confirm mode, when the cancel button or ESC is activated.

slots.name: default
slots.description: Content slot for dialog body (used in content mode)

slots.name: content
slots.description: Named slot for custom content in confirm mode

examples:
Confirm mode (with buttons):
<schmancy-dialog
  title="Delete item?"
  message="This action cannot be undone."
  confirm-text="Delete"
  cancel-text="Keep"
></schmancy-dialog>

declarations.name: SchmancyDivider
declarations.description: Thin horizontal (or vertical) separator rule between sections of content.
declarations.summary: Semantic separator between groups — list items, menu sections, content blocks. Uses outline theme token.

platformPrimitive.note: Styled horizontal rule. Degrades to a native `<hr>` if the tag never registers.

declarations.name: SchmancyDropdown
declarations.description: Anchored floating dropdown — a generic "show this content relative to that trigger" primitive. Unlike schmancy-menu (which uses the dialog service and is list-shaped), dropdown is a low-level popover anchored with Floating UI. Use when you want a custom-shaped overlay tied to a specific trigger element without the menu semantics.
declarations.summary: Prefer schmancy-menu for action lists, schmancy-autocomplete for type-ahead, schmancy-tooltip for hover hints. Reach for schmancy-dropdown when none of those fit — custom filters, color pickers, inline forms anchored to a trigger.

platformPrimitive.note: Anchored via Floating UI (autoUpdate, flip, shift). Degrades to inline content if the tag never registers — loses positioning but content stays accessible.

attributes.name: open
attributes.description: Whether the dropdown is currently open

attributes.name: placement
attributes.description: Placement of the dropdown relative to the trigger

attributes.name: distance
attributes.description: Offset distance in pixels

slots.name: trigger
slots.description: The element that triggers the dropdown
Default slot for the dropdown content

declarations.name: SchmancyDropdownContent
declarations.description: Content panel for a schmancy-dropdown — a styled positioned surface. Always nested inside schmancy-dropdown and placed alongside the trigger slot.
declarations.summary: Nest this inside schmancy-dropdown (not in the trigger slot). Use the `content` CSS part to customize backgrounds / shadows / padding without shadow-root piercing.

platformPrimitive.note: Positioned panel with theme-aware styling. Degrades to an inline div if the tag never registers.

attributes.name: width
attributes.description: Width of the dropdown content

attributes.name: maxHeight
attributes.description: Maximum height of the dropdown content

attributes.name: shadow
attributes.description: Whether to render with a shadow

attributes.name: radius
attributes.description: Border radius style

cssParts.name: content
cssParts.description: The inner wrapper element; style to override panel backgrounds, shadows, padding, or borders without shadow-root piercing.

declarations.name: SchmancyExpandRoot
declarations.description: Container for schmancy-expand children — coordinates mutual-exclusion so only one child is open at a time. Also renders the portal panel that the active child expands into.
declarations.summary: Always wrap a group of schmancy-expand children. Without a root, each schmancy-expand behaves independently (which is usually not what you want — prefer schmancy-details for that).

platformPrimitive.note: Coordinating wrapper. Degrades to a plain div if the tag never registers — children fall back to independent `<details>` behavior.

declarations.name: SchmancyExpand
declarations.description: Accordion-style section — expands on click, coordinates with siblings via schmancy-expand-root to close any sibling when a new one opens. Only one schmancy-expand can be open at a time within the same root.
declarations.summary: Use for grouped progressive-disclosure where only one section should be open at a time. Prefer schmancy-details when sections should be independent.

platformPrimitive.note: Schmancy-skinned accordion section. Degrades to `<details>` if the tag never registers — loses mutual-exclusion behavior but stays functional.

examples:
<schmancy-expand-root>
  <schmancy-expand summary="Billing">Billing form…</schmancy-expand>
  <schmancy-expand summary="Shipping">Shipping form…</schmancy-expand>
  <schmancy-expand summary="Review">Order review…</schmancy-expand>
</schmancy-expand-root>

declarations.name: SchmancyForm
declarations.description: Ergonomic wrapper around a native `<form>`. Children are reparented into a light-DOM `<form>` on connection so form-associated custom elements resolve `internals.form` via native DOM ancestry.  - Form-associated custom elements (FACE) resolve their `internals.form`   correctly via native DOM ancestry. - `new FormData(form)` collects values from every FACE + native control   without any manual walking. - `form.reset()` triggers `formResetCallback()` on every FACE. - `form.reportValidity()` runs native validation UI. - `<button type="submit">` and `<schmancy-button type="submit">` both   submit the form via the native submitter pipeline.  This component exists only to translate the native `submit` / `reset` events into the Schmancy event shape (`detail: FormData`). All heavy lifting is the platform's.
declarations.summary: Always wrap form-associated schmancy components in schmancy-form (or a native `<form>`) so `new FormData(form)` just works.

platformPrimitive.note: Light-DOM native `<form>` element. Degrades to a `<form>` if the tag never registers — same semantics, just no CustomEvent translation.

events.name: submit
events.description: `CustomEvent<FormData>` emitted when the form is submitted.

events.name: reset
events.description: Emitted after the underlying form resets.

examples:
<schmancy-form @submit=${(e) => console.log(Object.fromEntries(e.detail))}>
  <schmancy-input name="email" type="email" required></schmancy-input>
  <schmancy-input name="password" type="password" required></schmancy-input>
  <schmancy-button type="submit" variant="filled">Sign in</schmancy-button>
</schmancy-form>

attributes.name: fill
attributes.description: Fill value for the icon (0-1) 0 = outlined, 1 = filled

attributes.name: weight
attributes.description: Weight value for the icon (100-700) Controls the thickness of the icon strokes

attributes.name: grade
attributes.description: Grade value for the icon (-50-200) Adjusts the visual weight/grade

attributes.name: variant
attributes.description: Icon variant style

attributes.name: size
attributes.description: Size of the icon - M3 aligned tokens or custom string Tokens: 'xxs' (12px), 'xs' (16px), 'sm' (20px), 'md' (24px), 'lg' (32px) Custom: any CSS size string like '48px', '2rem'  When this icon is a descendant of `<schmancy-button>`, the button's `size` wins (via `SchmancyButtonSizeContext`). The local `size` only applies when there is no ancestor button.

attributes.name: icon
attributes.description: Icon name - use this instead of slot content to prevent translation breaking icons. When set, this takes precedence over slot content. Example: <schmancy-icon icon="delete"></schmancy-icon>

cssParts.name: icon
cssParts.description: The inner `<span>` carrying the Material Symbols glyph.

declarations.name: SchmancyIframe
declarations.description: Renders an HTML fragment inside a sandboxed, auto-sizing iframe.

attributes.name: html
attributes.description: HTML body fragment to render inside the iframe

attributes.name: css
attributes.description: Additional CSS injected after the base styles (consumer-specific)

attributes.name: baseCss
attributes.description: Base document CSS (font, spacing, resets). Override for fully custom styling

attributes.name: sandbox
attributes.description: iframe sandbox attribute

attributes.name: minHeight
attributes.description: Minimum height in pixels

declarations.name: SchmancyInput
declarations.description: Single-line text input — the primary form-text primitive. Form-associated via ElementInternals, so it participates in native `<form>` submission, validation, and reset without additional wiring.
declarations.summary: Text input with Material Design styling, native form integration, and RxJS-debounced input/change/enter events.

platformPrimitive.note: Schmancy-skinned native `<input>`. Degrades to `<input class="…">` styled via Tailwind if the tag never registers.

attributes.name: value
attributes.description: Override value to be string only for input element

attributes.name: type
attributes.description: The type of input. (e.g. 'text', 'password', 'email', etc.)

attributes.name: pattern
attributes.description: Pattern validation attribute.

attributes.name: clickable
attributes.description: If true, we visually show a pointer cursor even if readOnly.

attributes.name: spellcheck
attributes.description: Whether browser spellcheck is enabled.

attributes.name: align
attributes.description: Text alignment within the input. - 'left' | 'center' | 'right'

attributes.name: inputmode
attributes.description: inputmode attribute (affects on-screen keyboards in mobile).

attributes.name: autofocus
attributes.description: If true, auto-focus this input on first render.

attributes.name: autocomplete
attributes.description: Autocomplete/autofill hints.

attributes.name: tabIndex
attributes.description: tabIndex for focusing by tab key. Typically 0 or -1.

attributes.name: size
attributes.description: The size of the input. - 'xxs': Ultra-compact size (24px) - for menu cards - 'xs': Extra small, very compact size (32px) - 'sm': Small, compact size (40px) - 'md': Medium size (default) (48px) - 'lg': Large size (56px)

attributes.name: validateOn
attributes.description: Controls when validation should show. - 'always' - Always show validation - 'touched' - Only show after field has been focused and then blurred - 'dirty' - Only show after value has changed - 'submitted' - Only show after form submission

attributes.name: list
attributes.description: For datalist support

declarations.name: SchmancyInputCompat
declarations.description: Register the component with the legacy tag name for backward compatibility

declarations.name: SchmancyKbd
declarations.description: Renders a keyboard shortcut hint (e.g. `⌘ K`, `Ctrl+C`). Semantically a `<kbd>` element for screen readers; visually styled as a pressed key.  Compose multiple instances for combinations: ```html <schmancy-kbd>⌘</schmancy-kbd> + <schmancy-kbd>K</schmancy-kbd> ```

declarations.name: SchmancyListItem
declarations.description: Single row in a schmancy-list — supports leading icon/avatar, main content, trailing actions. Clickable via click event, or used as a schmancy-menu-item's visual base.
declarations.summary: Prefer nesting inside schmancy-list for consistent spacing/surface. Use `href` to make the row a navigation target (renders as `<a>`), or a click handler for in-app actions.

platformPrimitive.note: Styled `<li>` or `<a>` depending on `href`. Degrades to a plain list item if the tag never registers.

declarations.name: List
declarations.description: Wrapped list container — holds schmancy-list-item children on a themed surface. Optionally scrollable.
declarations.summary: Use for vertical lists of similarly-shaped items: settings entries, menu items, contact lists, notification lists. Pair with schmancy-list-item children.

platformPrimitive.note: Styled list container. Degrades to a plain ul/div if the tag never registers.

attributes.name: surface
attributes.description: Defines the type or color of the surface used by the component. This value is provided to descendant components via context.

attributes.name: fill
attributes.description: Determines the fill style of the underlying surface.

attributes.name: elevation
attributes.description: Defines the elevation level of the surface.

slots.description: The default slot for list items.

declarations.name: SchmancyEmailEditor
declarations.description: Email editor component with rich text formatting and file attachments  Features: - Markdown formatting toolbar - Image upload and insertion - File attachments with drag & drop - Layout templates (columns, sidebars, image rows) - Real-time character/word count

attributes.name: subject
attributes.description: Email subject

attributes.name: body
attributes.description: Email body content (markdown)

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Disable all interactions

attributes.name: attachments
attributes.description: Email attachments

attributes.name: config
attributes.description: Configuration for upload handlers

attributes.name: templates
attributes.description: Available email templates

declarations.name: SchmancyEmailLayoutSelector
declarations.description: Email layout selector component for choosing email templates  Simple horizontal row of 5 layout options using Schmancy components only.

events.name: layout-select
events.description: When a layout is selected with {detail: {layout: string}}

declarations.name: SchmancyEmailRecipients
declarations.description: Email recipients management component with floating boat interface  Features: - Floating boat UI that can be minimized/expanded - CSV import with drag & drop - Multiple import sources (configurable) - Bulk selection and management - Search and filtering - Email validation

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Disable all interactions

attributes.name: recipients
attributes.description: All available recipients

attributes.name: selectedRecipients
attributes.description: Currently selected recipients

attributes.name: enableCsvImport
attributes.description: Enable CSV import functionality

attributes.name: enableDragDrop
attributes.description: Enable drag and drop for files

attributes.name: title
attributes.description: Panel title

attributes.name: emptyStateTitle
attributes.description: Empty state title

attributes.name: emptyStateMessage
attributes.default: 'Import from sources or upload a CSV'
attributes.description: Empty state message

attributes.name: csvParser
attributes.description: CSV parser function (optional)

declarations.name: SchmancyEmailTemplatePicker
declarations.description: Email template picker content component (for use inside sheets)  Features: - Grid layout for template preview - Search/filter templates - Category filtering - Inline preview with direct selection - Single-click template selection

attributes.name: templates
attributes.description: Available templates

declarations.name: SchmancyEmailViewer
declarations.description: Email viewer component showing formatted HTML and plain text versions  Features: - HTML and plain text preview modes - Email client-compatible styling - Layout parsing (columns, sidebars, image rows) - Markdown to HTML conversion - Attachment display - Email header simulation - Character/word statistics

attributes.name: subject
attributes.description: Email subject

attributes.name: body
attributes.description: Email body content (markdown)

attributes.name: attachments
attributes.description: Email attachments

attributes.name: recipients
attributes.description: Selected recipients for preview

attributes.name: fromAddress
attributes.description: From address for preview

attributes.name: toAddress
attributes.description: To address for preview (uses first recipient if not provided)

declarations.name: SchmancyMailbox
declarations.description: Main mailbox component that orchestrates the entire email composition flow  Features: - Responsive layout with composer and preview panels - Recipients management with floating boat interface - Template selection and content editing - Attachment handling - Email validation and sending

attributes.name: config
attributes.description: Configuration for endpoints and handlers

attributes.name: templates
attributes.description: Available email templates

attributes.name: importSources
attributes.description: Import sources for recipients panel

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Disable all interactions

attributes.name: recipientsTitle
attributes.description: Recipients panel title

attributes.name: recipientsEmptyTitle
attributes.description: Empty state title for recipients

attributes.name: recipientsEmptyMessage
attributes.default: 'Import from sources or upload a CSV'
attributes.description: Empty state message for recipients

attributes.name: enableCsvImport
attributes.description: Enable CSV import functionality

attributes.name: enableDragDrop
attributes.description: Enable drag and drop for files

declarations.name: SchmancyMap
declarations.description: `<schmancy-map>` component  A Google Maps component with an intuitive API for displaying interactive or static maps. Supports both address strings (with automatic geocoding) and precise coordinates.

attributes.name: address
attributes.description: Simple address string that automatically geocodes to display the location. Takes precedence over latitude/longitude if both are provided.

attributes.name: latitude
attributes.description: Latitude coordinate for precise location. Used when address is not provided.

attributes.name: longitude
attributes.description: Longitude coordinate for precise location. Used when address is not provided.

attributes.name: zoom
attributes.description: Map zoom level. Higher numbers show more detail.

attributes.name: height
attributes.description: Height of the map with CSS unit (e.g., "400px", "50vh").

attributes.name: marker
attributes.description: Whether to show a marker at the location.

attributes.name: markerTitle
attributes.description: Tooltip text for the location marker.

attributes.name: type
attributes.description: Map display type. Options: "roadmap", "satellite", "hybrid", "terrain"

attributes.name: interactive
attributes.description: Whether users can interact with the map (pan, zoom, click).

attributes.name: controls
attributes.description: Whether to show map controls (zoom buttons, fullscreen, etc.).

attributes.name: apiKey
attributes.description: Google Maps API key. Required for the map to load.

examples:
<!-- Simple address -->
<schmancy-map address="Times Square, New York"></schmancy-map>

<!-- Satellite view -->
<schmancy-map address="Grand Canyon" type="satellite" height="500px"></schmancy-map>

<!-- Static map -->
<schmancy-map address="Eiffel Tower, Paris" interactive="false" controls="false"></schmancy-map>

declarations.name: SchmancyMenuItem
declarations.description: Single item inside a schmancy-menu. Auto-dismisses the menu dialog on click — attach your action handler to `@click` and it just works.
declarations.summary: Always nested inside schmancy-menu. The click handler runs before the dialog closes, so `@click=${() => doThing()}` is the full pattern.

platformPrimitive.note: Wraps schmancy-list-item with auto-dismiss. Degrades to a styled `<button role="menuitem">` if the tag never registers.

slots.description: The item label and optional icons.

declarations.name: SchmancyMenu
declarations.description: Floating menu — a trigger button + a list of schmancy-menu-item children that open as a positioned dialog on click.
declarations.summary: Use for dropdown menus attached to a button or icon — "More actions", "Account", row overflow menus in tables. Clicking a schmancy-menu-item inside auto-dismisses; custom components slotted inside must call `$dialog.dismiss()` themselves.

platformPrimitive.note: Trigger + floating listbox dialog. Degrades to a native `<select>` or inline list if the tag never registers.

slots.name: default
slots.description: Menu items or any custom component to display in dialog

declarations.name: SchmancyMetric
declarations.description: KPI metric — a label + value pair for dashboards, with optional trend + delta indicators.
declarations.summary: The building block of dashboards and meta bars. Pass `label` + `value` for a basic stat; add `trend` / `delta` for the delta-from-last-period pattern. Use multiple metrics side-by-side with Tailwind flex/grid utilities.

platformPrimitive.note: Styled `<div>` with two text lines + optional trend arrow. Degrades to a plain div+spans if the tag never registers.

attributes.name: label
attributes.description: Upper-case caption shown above the value.

attributes.name: value
attributes.description: Primary metric value, rendered large. Pre-format numbers/currency yourself.

attributes.name: trend
attributes.description: Optional trend direction. Controls the color + arrow on the delta pill.

attributes.name: delta
attributes.description: Optional delta copy displayed in a pill next to the value (e.g. `+12%`).

attributes.name: size
attributes.description: Size scale affecting label + value typography.

slots.description: Optional custom value rendering (overrides the `value` attribute if present).

slots.name: label
slots.description: Optional custom label rendering (overrides the `label` attribute if present).

cssParts.name: delta
cssParts.description: The delta pill (only when `delta` is set).

examples:
<schmancy-metric label="In flight" value="4"></schmancy-metric>
<schmancy-metric label="Open value" value="€165,900" trend="up" delta="+12%"></schmancy-metric>
<schmancy-metric label="Error rate" value="0.3%" trend="down" delta="-0.1%"></schmancy-metric>

declarations.name: SchmancyDrawerAppbar
declarations.description: Top app bar region inside schmancy-nav-drawer — the horizontal strip above the content area that typically holds the page title, hamburger trigger, and contextual actions.
declarations.summary: Always nested inside schmancy-nav-drawer. Holds the page-level title + top-right actions. On mobile, the drawer's hamburger button renders inside this region.

platformPrimitive.note: Styled horizontal bar. Degrades to a plain header element if the tag never registers.

declarations.name: SchmancyNavigationDrawerContent
declarations.description: Main content region inside schmancy-nav-drawer — typically hosts the router outlet or the page's body content.
declarations.summary: Always nested inside schmancy-nav-drawer. Scrollable by default; propagates scroll events up so the drawer can collapse app-bar on scroll.

platformPrimitive.note: Scrollable `<main>`. Degrades to a plain scrollable div if the tag never registers.

declarations.name: SchmancyNavigationDrawer
declarations.description: Responsive navigation drawer — a left sidebar that becomes a hamburger-triggered modal overlay on narrow viewports. Composes schmancy-nav-drawer-navbar (the nav rail), schmancy-nav-drawer-appbar (the top bar), and schmancy-nav-drawer-content (the main region).
declarations.summary: The app-shell layout primitive. Wrap your whole app in this when you want "persistent sidebar on desktop, drawer on mobile" behavior. Mode auto-switches at the breakpoint.

platformPrimitive.note: Flex layout with viewport-width mode switching. Degrades to a stack of plain divs if the tag never registers.

attributes.name: breakpoint
attributes.description: The breakpoint for the sidebar based on Tailwind CSS breakpoints. Accepts: "sm", "md", "lg", or "xl".

events.name: schmancy-drawer-state
events.description: When the drawer open/close state changes on mobile.

examples:
<schmancy-nav-drawer>
  <schmancy-nav-drawer-navbar>
    <!-- nav items, typically schmancy-list-item links -->
  </schmancy-nav-drawer-navbar>
  <schmancy-nav-drawer-appbar>App title</schmancy-nav-drawer-appbar>
  <schmancy-nav-drawer-content>
    <!-- router outlet / page content -->
  </schmancy-nav-drawer-content>
</schmancy-nav-drawer>

declarations.name: SchmancyNavigationDrawerSidebar
declarations.description: Sidebar region inside schmancy-nav-drawer — the persistent-on-desktop / modal-on-mobile nav rail.
declarations.summary: Always nested inside schmancy-nav-drawer. On desktop: pinned left sidebar. On mobile: modal overlay triggered by the hamburger button in schmancy-nav-drawer-appbar.

platformPrimitive.note: Sidebar `<nav>` with responsive open/close behavior. Degrades to a plain sidebar div if the tag never registers.

declarations.name: SchmancyNavigationBarItem
declarations.description: Single destination inside schmancy-navigation-bar — an icon + optional label representing one primary app destination.
declarations.summary: Always nested inside schmancy-navigation-bar. Use `icon` attr for a Material Symbols glyph or slot=icon for custom content.

platformPrimitive.note: Styled navigation target. Degrades to a plain `<button>` if the tag never registers.

attributes.name: icon
attributes.description: Icon name for the navigation item (Material Symbols Outlined)

attributes.name: label
attributes.description: Label text for the navigation item

attributes.name: badge
attributes.description: Badge content (can be a number or short text)

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Whether this item is disabled

attributes.name: hideLabels
attributes.description: Whether to hide labels (controlled by parent navigation bar)

declarations.name: SchmancyNavigationBar
declarations.description: Bottom navigation bar — Material Design 3 horizontal nav for mobile primary destinations (3–7 items). Auto-hides in fullscreen mode.
declarations.summary: Use for mobile primary navigation (home, search, favorites, settings). For persistent desktop nav, prefer schmancy-navigation-rail or schmancy-nav-drawer. Consumer is responsible for fixed-bottom positioning via class.

platformPrimitive.note: Styled horizontal nav with schmancy-navigation-bar-item children. Degrades to a plain flex container if the tag never registers.

attributes.name: hideLabels
attributes.description: Hide labels and show only icons

attributes.name: elevation
attributes.description: Elevation level for the navigation bar

attributes.name: hideOnScroll
attributes.description: Hide navigation bar on scroll down, show on scroll up

slots.description: Default slot for navigation bar items

examples:
<!-- Fixed at bottom (typical usage) -->
<schmancy-navigation-bar activeIndex="0" class="fixed bottom-0 left-0 right-0">
  <schmancy-navigation-bar-item icon="home" label="Home"></schmancy-navigation-bar-item>
  <schmancy-navigation-bar-item icon="search" label="Search"></schmancy-navigation-bar-item>
  <schmancy-navigation-bar-item icon="favorite" label="Favorites"></schmancy-navigation-bar-item>
  <schmancy-navigation-bar-item icon="settings" label="Settings"></schmancy-navigation-bar-item>
</schmancy-navigation-bar>
Content

declarations.name: SchmancyNavigationRailItem
declarations.description: Single destination inside schmancy-navigation-rail — vertically-stacked icon + optional label. @see https://m3.material.io/components/navigation-rail/overview
declarations.summary: Always nested inside schmancy-navigation-rail. Use `icon` attr for a Material Symbols glyph, or slot=icon for custom content. Supports a `badge` slot for notification dots / counts.

platformPrimitive.note: Styled vertical navigation target. Degrades to a plain `<button>` if the tag never registers.

attributes.name: icon
attributes.description: Icon name (Material Symbols icon)

attributes.name: label
attributes.description: Label text for the navigation item

attributes.name: value
attributes.description: Value associated with this item (useful for routing)

attributes.name: badge
attributes.description: Badge text or number to display

attributes.name: badgeVariant
attributes.description: Badge variant

attributes.name: show-label
attributes.description: Whether to show the label (controlled by parent rail)

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Whether this item is disabled

attributes.name: nested
attributes.description: Whether this is a nested item (sub-navigation)

attributes.name: group
attributes.description: Whether this item represents a group separator

slots.name: icon
slots.description: Slot for the navigation item icon (e.g., schmancy-icon)

examples:
<!-- Using 'selected' alias -->
<schmancy-navigation-rail-item
  icon="settings"
  label="Settings"
  value="/settings"
  selected>
</schmancy-navigation-rail-item>

declarations.name: SchmancyNavigationRail
declarations.description: Vertical navigation rail — Material Design 3 compact left-side nav for desktop / tablet layouts with 3–7 primary destinations. Auto-hides in fullscreen mode. @see https://m3.material.io/components/navigation-rail/overview  @element schmancy-navigation-rail @summary Use as the desktop counterpart of schmancy-navigation-bar: same destinations, different form factor. Prefer schmancy-nav-drawer when you also want a drawer + app-bar combo.

attributes.name: label-visibility
attributes.description: When to show labels for navigation items 'all' - Always show labels for all items 'selected' - Only show label for selected item 'none' - Never show labels @default 'all'

attributes.name: alignment
attributes.description: Alignment of navigation items @default 'top'

attributes.name: showTooltips
attributes.description: Show tooltips when labels are hidden @default true

attributes.name: keyboardNavigation
attributes.description: Enable keyboard navigation @default true

attributes.name: expanded
attributes.description: Whether the navigation rail is expanded @default false

examples:
<schmancy-navigation-rail activeIndex="0">
  <schmancy-icon-button slot="fab" variant="filled">
    <schmancy-icon>add</schmancy-icon>
  </schmancy-icon-button>
  <schmancy-navigation-rail-item icon="home" label="Home"></schmancy-navigation-rail-item>
  <schmancy-navigation-rail-item icon="search" label="Search"></schmancy-navigation-rail-item>
  <schmancy-navigation-rail-item icon="settings" label="Settings"></schmancy-navigation-rail-item>
</schmancy-navigation-rail>
@platform nav - Vertical styled nav. Degrades to a plain vertical flex container if the tag never registers.
@slot fab - Slot for a floating action button at the top
@slot menu - Slot for a menu icon or button below the FAB
@slot header - Custom header content slot
@slot footer - Custom footer content slot
@slot - Default slot for navigation rail items

@fires navigate - When a navigation item is selected
@fires menu-click - When the menu button is clicked
@fires fab-click - When the FAB is clicked

@csspart rail - The main rail container
@csspart header - The header section
@csspart nav - The navigation items container
@csspart footer - The footer section

<schmancy-navigation-rail activeIndex="0">
  <schmancy-button slot="fab" variant="filled" aria-label="Compose">
    <schmancy-icon>add</schmancy-icon>
  </schmancy-button>
  <schmancy-button slot="menu" variant="text" aria-label="Menu">
    <schmancy-icon>menu</schmancy-icon>
  </schmancy-button>
  <schmancy-navigation-rail-item icon="home" label="Home"></schmancy-navigation-rail-item>
  <schmancy-navigation-rail-item icon="search" label="Search"></schmancy-navigation-rail-item>
  <schmancy-navigation-rail-item icon="favorite" label="Favorites" badge="3"></schmancy-navigation-rail-item>
  <schmancy-navigation-rail-item icon="settings" label="Settings"></schmancy-navigation-rail-item>
</schmancy-navigation-rail>

declarations.name: $notify
declarations.description: Global notification (toast) utility. Fire-and-forget API for success, error, info, and warning toasts, plus a low-level `show` for custom notifications.
declarations.summary: Toast notifications — success, error, info, warning.

declarations.name: SchmancyOption
declarations.description: `schmancy-option` is an option element for schmancy-select and schmancy-autocomplete components.

attributes.name: value
attributes.description: The value of the option, will be used when selected.

attributes.name: label
attributes.description: The human-readable label for the option.

attributes.name: selected
attributes.description: Whether the option is currently selected.

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Whether the option is disabled.

attributes.name: group
attributes.description: Optional group this option belongs to (for option grouping).

attributes.name: icon
attributes.description: Optional icon or image to display before the label.

declarations.name: SchmancyOverlay
declarations.description: The single overlay element. Hosts a native `<dialog>` in its shadow root for centered/sheet layouts; for anchored, we stay modeless by using `<dialog>` with .show() (non-modal) + explicit positioning.  Public lifecycle: the service calls `open()` to mount content and animate in, `close(reason)` to animate out and dismiss. The element dispatches `overlay-close` when closed; the service listens and resolves the caller's Observable.

declarations.name: SchmancyOverlayPromptBody
declarations.description: Internal body component used by the `confirm()` / `prompt()` sugar.  Minimal, dependency-free — plain HTML buttons / input so this file doesn't need to import schmancy-form / schmancy-button (avoids the risk of circular module graphs during early imports of $overlay).  Emits a `close` CustomEvent with the typed result; the overlay picks that up as the primary return channel. For custom-styled confirms, callers pass their own component to `show()`.

attributes.name: mode
attributes.description: Presence of `mode` switches between confirm (boolean) and prompt (string).

declarations.name: SchmancyPage
declarations.description: Mobile-first page container — fills remaining viewport height, suppresses double-tap zoom / pull-to-refresh / rubber-banding. Lays children in a CSS grid whose row template is `rows`.
declarations.summary: The root of any app view — wraps header / main / footer children in a full-viewport grid. Use rows="auto_1fr_auto" to make the middle child scroll while header/footer stay pinned.

platformPrimitive.note: Full-height CSS-grid container. Degrades to a plain div if the tag never registers — children still flow vertically but without the height fill and gesture suppression.

attributes.name: rows
attributes.description: Custom grid-template-rows using underscores (e.g. "1fr_2fr_auto")

declarations.name: RadioButton
declarations.description: Single radio button — always rendered as a child of `<schmancy-radio-group>`, never standalone.
declarations.summary: Low-level primitive. Use schmancy-radio-group and pass `.options` for the common path; only instantiate schmancy-radio-button directly when you need per-button custom rendering.

platformPrimitive.note: Schmancy-skinned `<input type="radio">` semantics. Degrades to native radio if the tag never registers.

declarations.name: RadioGroup
declarations.description: Radio-button group — single-select from a static list of mutually-exclusive options. Form-associated.
declarations.summary: Use for 2–5 mutually-exclusive options where all should stay visible ("Shipping: standard / express / overnight"). Prefer schmancy-select when the list grows.

platformPrimitive.note: Renders schmancy-radio-button children. Degrades to a fieldset with native `<input type="radio" name="…">` siblings if the tag never registers.

examples:
<schmancy-radio-group
  name="shipping"
  label="Shipping"
  .options=${[
    { label: 'Standard (5 days)', value: 'standard' },
    { label: 'Express (2 days)', value: 'express' },
    { label: 'Overnight', value: 'overnight' },
  ]}
></schmancy-radio-group>

events.name: change
events.description: Fires on value change with { value: number }

declarations.name: SchmancySelect
declarations.description: Dropdown selector — single or multi-select from a list of `<schmancy-option>` children. Form-associated.
declarations.summary: Material Design dropdown with type-to-filter, keyboard nav, single or multi-select. Options are declared as `<schmancy-option>` children; value / values props sync with selection.

platformPrimitive.note: Floating-UI-positioned listbox. Degrades to native `<select>` styled via Tailwind if the tag never registers, though multi-select UX is lost.

examples:
<schmancy-select name="priority" label="Priority" value="medium">
  <schmancy-option value="low">Low</schmancy-option>
  <schmancy-option value="medium">Medium</schmancy-option>
  <schmancy-option value="high">High</schmancy-option>
</schmancy-select>

declarations.name: sheet
declarations.description: Bottom / side sheet service. Mounts any element as a sheet with a chosen position (see `SchmancySheetPosition` enum).
declarations.summary: Imperative bottom / side sheet surface.

methods.summary: Mount an element as a sheet.
Dismiss a sheet by id, or the topmost.

declarations.name: SchmancySheet
declarations.description: Side-docked or bottom-docked panel — a dialog variant that slides in from an edge. Driven imperatively by the `sheet` service; rarely instantiated directly.
declarations.summary: Prefer `sheet.open({ component, position })` over placing this element declaratively — the service handles stacking, focus, close on outside-click, ESC, and router integration.

platformPrimitive.note: Positioned-fixed panel with backdrop. Degrades to a `<dialog>` if the tag never registers — loses slide animation, keeps focus trap + dismiss.

events.name: close
events.description: When the sheet is dismissed (backdrop click, close button, ESC).

declarations.name: SchmancySkeleton
declarations.description: Placeholder shimmer surface shown while content loads. Renders a shape-configurable rectangle with a reduced-motion-aware shimmer.

attributes.name: type
attributes.description: Determines how this slide should be rendered. Defaults to 'content' if not provided.

attributes.name: src
attributes.description: Source for images or videos (if `type` is 'image' or 'video').

attributes.name: alt
attributes.description: Alternate text for images.

attributes.name: controls
attributes.description: Whether to show default video controls (if `type` is 'video').

attributes.name: autoplay
attributes.description: Whether the video should autoplay (if `type` is 'video').

attributes.name: loop
attributes.description: Whether the video should loop (if `type` is 'video').

attributes.name: muted
attributes.description: Whether the video is muted (if `type` is 'video').

attributes.name: fit
attributes.description: CSS `object-fit` property, applied to images/videos.

attributes.name: showArrows
attributes.description: If true, renders next/prev buttons

declarations.name: SchmancySplashScreen
declarations.description: Full-viewport splash overlay that dismisses once a minimum duration has elapsed and (optionally) an external `ready` signal has fired. The splash fades out while the underlying app content fades in.  Bring-your-own visuals: the `splash` slot is empty by default so the component pulls in no dependencies. Provide a logo, spinner, or animation from the consumer side.

attributes.name: min-duration
attributes.description: Minimum duration (ms) the splash layer stays visible. Prevents a flash when the app loads faster than expected.

attributes.name: auto
attributes.description: When true, the splash dismisses on the `minDuration` timer alone. When false (default), it additionally waits for an external ready signal (a `ready` event on this element, or a call to `this.ready()`).

attributes.name: initially-hidden
attributes.description: When true, the splash starts hidden. Use this for imperative control.

slots.name: splash
slots.description: Content rendered on the splash layer (logo, spinner, etc.).
Default slot for the actual app content, revealed once dismissed.

attributes.name: position
attributes.description: The step's position (1-based). This is used to compare against the container's current step to decide if it's "complete", "current", or "upcoming".

attributes.name: lockBack
attributes.description: NEW: Lock API to disable users from going back. When set to true, clicking on a previous (completed) step is ignored.

attributes.name: gap
attributes.description: Gap between steps. Maps to Tailwind gap classes.

declarations.name: SchmancySurface
declarations.description: Themed container — the root surface primitive. Sets background, text color, rounding, elevation, and (optionally) internal scroll. Provides a `SchmancySurfaceTypeContext` so descendants can adapt to the enclosing surface variant.
declarations.summary: Wrap a region of a page when you need it to pick up theme tokens (background + on-color + elevation). Nest surfaces to express Material Design's hierarchical color stacking.

platformPrimitive.note: Styled `<div>` with theme-driven background/color/elevation. Degrades to a plain `<div>` if the tag never registers — text stays readable, just loses theming.

attributes.name: type
attributes.description: Specifies the surface type for styling. Provided to descendant components via context.

slots.description: Default slot for projecting child content.

examples: Your scrollable content here

declarations.name: SchmancySwitch
declarations.description: Binary on/off control with immediate effect. Form-associated, keyboard-accessible, semantically a switch (ARIA role="switch"). Distinct from `schmancy-checkbox`: a switch represents an immediate state change, a checkbox represents a selection in a form to be submitted.
declarations.summary: Use when flipping the control takes effect right away (e.g. "Dark mode", "Enable notifications"). Prefer schmancy-checkbox for form submissions.

platformPrimitive.note: Accessible native `<button role="switch" aria-checked>` under the hood. No native HTML element exists; falls back to a styled checkbox if the tag never registers.

events.name: change
events.description: `CustomEvent<{ value: boolean }>` when the state changes.

declarations.name: SchmancyDataTable
declarations.description: SchmancyDataTable is a generic data table component. It supports sorting, filtering, and custom rendering of rows.

events.name: tab-changed
events.description: The event fired when the tab is changed

slots.description: The content of the tab group

declarations.name: SchmancyTextarea
declarations.description: Multi-line text input with auto-resize and form integration. Form-associated.
declarations.summary: Textarea for freeform text — notes, descriptions, messages. Auto-grows with content up to a maxlength.

platformPrimitive.note: Schmancy-skinned native `<textarea>`. Degrades to styled native `<textarea>` if the tag never registers.

attributes.name: label
attributes.description: The label of the control.

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: The name of the control.

attributes.name: placeholder
attributes.description: The placeholder of the control.

attributes.name: value
attributes.description: The value of the control.

attributes.name: minlength
attributes.description: The minlength attribute of the control.

attributes.name: maxlength
attributes.description: The maxlength attribute of the control.

attributes.name: cols
attributes.description: The number of columns (width) of the control.

attributes.name: rows
attributes.description: The number of rows (height) of the control. When not set, the textarea auto-sizes to fit its content via field-sizing: content.

attributes.name: fillHeight
attributes.description: Makes the textarea fill the height of its container.

attributes.name: autoHeight
attributes.description: Automatically adjusts height based on content.

attributes.name: resize
attributes.description: Controls whether the textarea can be resized by the user.

attributes.name: wrap
attributes.description: Specifies how the text in a text area is to be wrapped when submitted in a form.

attributes.name: dirname
attributes.description: The dirname attribute of the control.

attributes.name: autofocus
attributes.description: The autofocus attribute of the control.

declarations.name: SchmancyThemeAudioPlayer
declarations.description: Mood Audio Player - Generates emotional sounds based on mood and theme Integrates with the Sound Service for consistent sound management

declarations.name: SchmancyThemeControllerBoat
declarations.description: BOAT UX theme controller - Wraps the base theme controller in a schmancy-boat component. Provides a floating, draggable container for theme controls.
examples:
<schmancy-theme-controller-boat></schmancy-theme-controller-boat>

<!-- With custom colors -->
<schmancy-theme-controller-boat
  .customColors="${[
    { name: 'Brand', value: '#FF5722', category: 'primary' }
  ]}"
></schmancy-theme-controller-boat>

declarations.name: SchmancyThemeController
declarations.description: Base theme controller component providing color and scheme controls. Can be used standalone without any specific UI container.
examples:
<schmancy-theme-controller></schmancy-theme-controller>

<!-- With custom colors -->
<schmancy-theme-controller
  .customColors="${[
    { name: 'Brand', value: '#FF5722', category: 'primary' }
  ]}"
></schmancy-theme-controller>

declarations.name: SchmancyThemeComponent
declarations.description: Theme provider — generates a Material 3 palette from a seed color, resolves light/dark scheme, and publishes the token set to descendants as CSS custom properties (var(--schmancy-sys-color-…)).
declarations.summary: Always wrap your app root in a `<schmancy-theme root scheme="auto" color="#…">`. Nest additional `<schmancy-theme>` blocks to override theming for a subtree.

platformPrimitive.note: Styled `<div>` that publishes theme tokens via inline `--schmancy-sys-color-*` custom properties. Degrades to a plain div if the tag never registers — children lose theming and fall back to browser defaults.

attributes.name: color
attributes.description: Primary color for the theme in hex format.

attributes.name: scheme
attributes.description: Color scheme for the theme.

attributes.name: root
attributes.description: Whether this theme should be applied at the root level (document.body).

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description: Locale for number/date formatting. Overrides navigator.language when set.

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: Unique name for this theme instance (used for session storage). If not provided, will be generated from DOM path.

attributes.name: theme
attributes.description: Theme configuration object containing all theme variables.

examples:
<!-- Root theme provider -->
<schmancy-theme root scheme="auto" color="#6200ee">
  <your-app></your-app>
</schmancy-theme>

<!-- Nested theme for a specific section -->
<schmancy-theme scheme="dark" color="#2196f3">
  <schmancy-surface fill="all">
    <!-- Components here use the blue dark theme -->
  </schmancy-surface>
</schmancy-theme>

declarations.name: theme
declarations.description: Theme singleton. Reads and mutates the active theme (scheme, source color, locale); persists the user's scheme choice. Components subscribe via `theme.theme$` for changes.
declarations.summary: Theme service — active scheme, source color, locale.

methods.signature: theme$ - Observable<TSchmancyTheme>; emits on every theme change.

methods.summary: Toggle between light and dark schemes.

declarations.name: SchmancyTree
declarations.description: Expandable tree node — a recursive disclosure widget. One root slot, one default slot for child nodes. Each node can itself contain schmancy-tree children.
declarations.summary: Use for hierarchical navigation / file-explorer layouts. Each level is a schmancy-tree with a `root` slot (the parent label) and default slot (the children, which may be more schmancy-trees).

platformPrimitive.note: Recursive `<details>`-like disclosure. Degrades to a plain nested list if the tag never registers — loses expand/collapse but stays navigable.

attributes.name: open
attributes.description: Whether the tree’s children are visible

slots.name: root
slots.description: The root element of the tree

attributes.name: speed
attributes.description: Typing speed in milliseconds per character.

attributes.name: delay
attributes.description: Delay before typing starts (ms).

attributes.name: autoStart
attributes.description: Automatically start typing on initialization.

attributes.name: cursorChar
attributes.description: The cursor character.

attributes.name: deleteSpeed
attributes.description: Typing speed for deletions (ms per character).

attributes.name: once
attributes.description: Only animate once per session.

attributes.name: loop
attributes.description: Loop the animation infinitely (overrides once).

attributes.name: cyclePause
attributes.description: Default pause duration for cycling (ms).

attributes.name: editable
attributes.description: When true, the element becomes contenteditable and dispatches 'change' events on blur/Enter

attributes.name: value
attributes.description: The text value when in editable mode. Set via property binding: .value=${...}

attributes.name: placeholder
attributes.description: Placeholder shown when editable and empty

declarations.name: SchmancyVisuallyHidden
declarations.description: Hides content visually while keeping it in the accessibility tree. Use for screen-reader-only labels, supplemental descriptions, and live-region text that sighted users don't need to see.  Uses the WCAG-recommended clip pattern rather than `display: none` or `visibility: hidden` so assistive tech still reads the content.

slots.description: Content hidden from sighted users but exposed to assistive tech.

attributes.name: expandedWidth
attributes.description: Override the expanded panel width (e.g. '320px', '24rem'). Defaults to responsive sizing.

attributes.name: expandedHeight
attributes.description: Override the expanded panel height (e.g. '400px', '50vh').

attributes.name: lowered
attributes.description: When true, uses a lower elevation shadow in the collapsed state.

attributes.name: corner
attributes.description: Corner the window is anchored to.

attributes.name: resizable
attributes.description: When true, window can be resized by the user.

attributes.name: freePosition
attributes.description: When true, window stays at its dragged position instead of snapping to a corner.

attributes.name: visualState
attributes.description: Visual state of the window (minimized, normal, maximized).

attributes.name: minWidth
attributes.description: Minimum width in pixels.

attributes.name: minHeight
attributes.description: Minimum height in pixels.

attributes.name: open
attributes.description: Whether the body is expanded.

attributes.name: value

type.text: { cardName: string; cardNumber: string; expirationDate: string; cvv: string; }

attributes.description: The form data containing all payment card information

events.name: change
events.description: Fires when any field in the form changes

slots.description: Default slot for any additional content

declarations.name: SchmancyCountriesSelect
declarations.description: Country picker — type-ahead autocomplete over the ISO 3166-1 country list. Form-associated.
declarations.summary: Drop-in replacement for schmancy-autocomplete when the options are specifically "every country". Pre-seeds the list from countries.data.

platformPrimitive.note: Composes schmancy-autocomplete with a static options list. Value is the 2-letter ISO code.

declarations.name: SchmancyTimezonesSelect
declarations.description: Timezone picker — type-ahead autocomplete over the IANA tz database. Form-associated.
declarations.summary: Drop-in replacement for schmancy-autocomplete when the options are IANA timezone names. Value is the IANA identifier ("America/Los_Angeles").

platformPrimitive.note: Composes schmancy-autocomplete with a static IANA timezones list.

events.name: change
events.description: `SchmancyAutocompleteChangeEvent` with `{ value: string }` (the IANA tz name).

type.text: { xs?: string | number; sm?: string | number; md?: string | number; lg?: string | number; xl?: string | number; '2xl'?: string | number; }

declarations.name: SchmancyScroll
declarations.description: Scrollable container with debounced scroll events, horizontal/vertical direction, optional hidden scrollbar, and programmatic scrollTo via command events or refs.
declarations.summary: Use anywhere you'd reach for `overflow: auto` but also need debounced scroll events (for sticky headers, scroll spies, virtualization triggers) or the ability to drive scroll from elsewhere in the app by dispatching a schmancy-scroll-command event.

platformPrimitive.note: Styled scrollable `<div>`. Degrades to a plain scrollable div if the tag never registers — loses the debounced scroll event and the command-bus integration.

attributes.name: hide
attributes.description: Determines whether the scrollbar is hidden.  When `hide` is true, the host element's scrollbars are hidden in supported browsers using CSS.

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: Optional name identifier for the component. Used for targeting this specific component with global events.

attributes.name: direction
attributes.description: Direction of scrolling: vertical, horizontal, or both. - vertical: Only allows vertical scrolling - horizontal: Only allows horizontal scrolling - both: Allows both horizontal and vertical scrolling (default)

attributes.name: debounce
attributes.description: Debounce time in milliseconds for the scroll event. Higher values reduce the frequency of scroll events being dispatched.

events.name: scroll
events.description: Fired when scrolling occurs (with a configurable debounce)

slots.description: Default slot for content to be scrolled

attributes.name: flow
attributes.description: Flow property that determines the flex direction. Allowed values:  - Standard: 'row', 'row-reverse', 'col', 'col-reverse'  - Dense variants: 'row-dense', 'col-dense' (dense implies wrapping)

attributes.name: wrap
attributes.description: Flex-wrap options:  - 'wrap', 'nowrap', or 'wrap-reverse'

attributes.name: align
attributes.description: Align-items (vertical alignment of flex items):  - 'start', 'center', 'end', 'stretch', or 'baseline'

attributes.name: justify
attributes.description: Justify-content (horizontal distribution):  - 'start', 'center', 'end', 'between', 'around', or 'evenly'

attributes.name: content
attributes.description: Align-content (spacing between rows when wrapping):  - 'start', 'center', 'end', 'between', 'around', or 'evenly'

conventions: Every component extends `$LitElement`, not raw `LitElement`. Use the mixin from `@mhmo91/schmancy/mixins`.
RxJS subscriptions inside a component must end with `.pipe(takeUntil(this.disconnecting))` for automatic cleanup on disconnect.
Theme must wrap a surface: `<schmancy-theme>` provides design tokens, `<schmancy-surface>` paints the background and inherits text color.
Use `repeat()` from `lit/directives/repeat.js` for dynamic lists — never `.map()` inside `html```.
